mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is pinned near zero and CPIAUCSL is rising, real rates are deeply negative and liquidity conditions stay loose; this supports equity multiple expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with FEDFUNDS <= 0.25 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation above zero, or if Rank IC stays below 0.03 in a rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals slowing household income growth and weaker future spending, which should pressure equities lower over the next month as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20d SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: An upturn in RSAFS above its recent trend indicates stronger consumer demand and revenue momentum, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as sales growth feeds earnings revisions.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60d SPX returns are not more common after rising RSAFS trend observations than after falling ones, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO accelerates while CPIAUCSL stays contained, real activity is improving without an offsetting inflation shock, which supports cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform each single-series proxy alone on 20d forward returns, or if triggered instances do not show positive returns at least 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in HOUST after a prior slump marks a housing-led demand rebound, which lifts consumer wealth effects and cyclically sensitive earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by more than 8% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX returns after the HOUST trigger are not above unconditional median returns, or if the signal flips sign in a stable rolling 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL implies the central bank is behind inflation, tightening financial conditions in real terms and compressing equity valuations, which should drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases while CPIAUCSL 6-month inflation is positive and accelerating
failure_condition: Reject if the joint tightening-plus-inflation condition does not produce negative 20d returns more often than positive returns, or if the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power over either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market deterioration regime, falling PAYEMS together with rising UNRATE signals payroll contraction and weaker aggregate income, which should cause negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations reset lower.
failure_condition: Reject if months with declining PAYEMS and rising UNRATE do not lead to below-median 60d SPX returns, or if the joint signal is not negative in a rolling 2-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive but sales and employment are not confirming, the expansion is fragile and equity markets should underperform over the next week because the macro print is likely backward-looking rather than earnings-supportive.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2% while RSAFS 3-month growth <= 0 and PAYEMS 3-month change <= 0
failure_condition: Reject if the divergence condition fails to generate negative 5d SPX returns more often than not, or if the pattern has no measurable edge versus random timing in walk-forward tests.
